'''Paul Forber''' (born 29 April 1964) is an English former [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|sr}} or {{rlp|pr}} in the 1980s and 1990s. He spent most of his career with [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], making almost 250 appearances in 11 seasons at the club. He also spent several seasons with [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] before finishing his career with [[Workington Town]] and [[Rochdale Hornets]].

==Playing career==
Forber started his professional rugby league career with [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], signing for the club in May 1981. He made his first team debut a year later against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] during the [[1982 Kangaroo tour]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Paul 'Buffer' Forber; A home-grown packman who ran the ball in hard |url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/19106202.paul-buffer-forber-home-town-packman-ran-ball-hard/ |website=St Helens Star |access-date=4 November 2024 |date=21 February 2021}}</ref> He established a regular place in the first team during the [[1984–85 Rugby Football League season|1984–85 season]], and played for the club in the [[1984–85 Rugby League Premiership|1985 Premiership]] final, appearing as a substitute in the 36–16 win against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].

The following season, he represented [[Lancashire rugby league team|Lancashire]] in a "War of the Roses" match against [[Yorkshire rugby league team|Yorkshire]]. He was a non-playing substitute in the [[1987 Challenge Cup]] final defeat against [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]].

He played at {{rlp|sr}} in the [[1987–88 John Player Special Trophy]] victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]. He also played in the [[1989 Challenge Cup]] final, but was again on the losing side, this time against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].

Forber missed most of the [[1990–91 Rugby Football League season|1990–91 season]] due to injury,<ref>{{cite news |title=Keeping Faith In Saint Paul |url=https://www.newspapers.com/image/903514660 |work=Liverpool Echo |date=16 April 1991 |page=18 |via=[[Newspapers.com]] |url-access=subscription}}</ref> but returned the following season to help Saints win the [[1991–92 Lancashire Cup]] against [[Rochdale Hornets]].<ref>{{cite web |title=St Helens beat Rochdale Hornets in 1991/92 Lancashire Cup Final |url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/news/18951702.st-helens-beat-rochdale-hornets-1991-92-lancashire-cup-final/ |website=St Helens Star |access-date=5 November 2024 |date=17 December 2020}}</ref>

In January 1993, Forber was signed by [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] for a fee of £25,000.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Hadfield |first1=Dave |title=Rugby League: Hornets sell off Hall to Wigan |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-hornets-sell-off-hall-to-wigan-1478141.html |website=The Independent |access-date=4 November 2024 |date=12 January 1993}}</ref> He was released at the end of the 1998 season, and joined [[Workington Town]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Sporting Digest: Rugby league |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/sporting-digest-rugby-league-1075539.html |website=The Independent |access-date=4 November 2024 |date=22 January 1999}}</ref>

In 2023, Forber was inducted into the St Helens Hall of Fame.<ref>{{cite web |title=Annual Past Players Dinner Hosted for 2023 |url=https://www.saintsrlfccommunity.com/annual-past-players-dinner-hosted-for-2023/ |website=Saints Foundation |access-date=5 November 2024}}</ref>
